title: Ghent Conservatory

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Ghent Conservatory](/w/index.php?title=Ghent_Conservatory&redirect=no "Ghent Conservatory"))

Music college in Ghent, Belgium

| Royal Conservatory of Ghent | |
| --- | --- |
| *Koninklijk Conservatorium Gent* ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language")) | |
| Map | |
| Biezekapelstraat 9, Ghent   Belgium | |
| Information | |
| Type | Music conservatory |
| Established | 1835 |
| Language | Dutch, English |
| Website | <https://schoolofartsgent.be/en/> |

The **Royal Conservatory of Ghent** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Koninklijk Conservatorium Gent*) is a historic [conservatory](/wiki/Music_school "Music school") and a royally chartered musical institution in [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent"), Belgium. It is now a part of the [University College Ghent](/wiki/Hogeschool_Gent "Hogeschool Gent").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Conservatory_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Royal Conservatory of Ghent is a royally chartered musical institution, founded in 1835[[1]](#cite_note-1) under [King Leopold I](/wiki/Leopold_I_of_Belgium "Leopold I of Belgium"). The conservatory's founding director was [Martin-Joseph Mengal](/wiki/Martin-Joseph_Mengal "Martin-Joseph Mengal"). Other directors have included well-known Belgian composers such as [Adolphe Samuel](/wiki/Adolphe_Samuel "Adolphe Samuel") and [Émile Mathieu](/wiki/%C3%89mile_Mathieu_(composer) "Émile Mathieu (composer)"). The current dean is Filip Rathé.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The Royal Conservatory is one of four conservatories in [Flanders](/wiki/Flanders "Flanders") and eight in [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"). In addition to classical instrumental and vocal studies, it offers degrees in jazz, pop, music production, composition and instrument building.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It offers bachelor's and master's degrees (including an English master's degree in music[[4]](#cite_note-4)), and an Advanced Master of Contemporary Music. The school also offers post-graduate degrees in music as well as a European Postgraduate in Arts in Sound.

The school traditionally attaches great importance to the practice of chamber music. Since 2006, the school has mounted an annual opera production. It also has two main performance venues, the Miry Concert Hall and Club Telex.

In 1995, the Royal Conservatory was one of sixteen Belgian institutions merged into the [University College Ghent](/wiki/Hogeschool_Gent "Hogeschool Gent"). It provides training for about 480 students, with master's programs for drama and music. Since 2011, together with the [Royal Academy of Fine Arts (KASK)](/wiki/Royal_Academy_of_Fine_Arts_(KASK) "Royal Academy of Fine Arts (KASK)"), it forms part of the *School of Arts* of the University College Ghent.

## Notable alumni

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Conservatory_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notable alumni")]

Notable students and faculty at the conservatory have included [François-Auguste Gevaert](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois-Auguste_Gevaert "François-Auguste Gevaert"), who studied directly under Mengal in 1841, [Paul-Henri-Joseph Lebrun](/wiki/Paul-Henri-Joseph_Lebrun "Paul-Henri-Joseph Lebrun"), who studied here and became a professor, and [Edouard Potjes](/wiki/Edouard_Potjes "Edouard Potjes"), who served as professor of piano for 22 years. Other famous alumni include:

* [Julien Paul Blitz](/wiki/Julien_Paul_Blitz "Julien Paul Blitz")
* [Dirk Brossé](/wiki/Dirk_Bross%C3%A9 "Dirk Brossé")
* [Lucien Goethals](/wiki/Lucien_Goethals "Lucien Goethals")
* [Walter Hus](/wiki/Walter_Hus "Walter Hus")
* [Karel Miry](/wiki/Karel_Miry "Karel Miry")
* [Karel Paukert](/wiki/Karel_Paukert "Karel Paukert")
* [Annelies Van Parys](/wiki/Annelies_Van_Parys "Annelies Van Parys")
* [Jane Vignery](/wiki/Jane_Vignery "Jane Vignery")

## Directors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Conservatory_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Directors")]

* [Martin-Joseph Mengal](/wiki/Martin-Joseph_Mengal "Martin-Joseph Mengal") 1835–1851
* [Jean Andries](/w/index.php?title=Jean_Andries&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean Andries (page does not exist)") 1798–1872
* [Adolphe Samuel](/wiki/Adolphe_Samuel "Adolphe Samuel") 1871–1898
* [Emile Mathieu](/wiki/Emile_Mathieu_(composer) "Emile Mathieu (composer)") 1898–1924
* [Martin Lunssens](/w/index.php?title=Martin_Lunssens&action=edit&redlink=1 "Martin Lunssens (page does not exist)") 1924–1936
* [Jules-Toussaint de Sutter](/w/index.php?title=Jules-Toussaint_de_Sutter&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jules-Toussaint de Sutter (page does not exist)") 1936–1954
* [Léon Torck](/w/index.php?title=L%C3%A9on_Torck&action=edit&redlink=1 "Léon Torck (page does not exist)") 1954–1968
* [Gabriël Verschraegen](/w/index.php?title=Gabri%C3%ABl_Verschraegen&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gabriël Verschraegen (page does not exist)") 1968–1981
* Johan Huys 1981–1997
* Jan Rispens 1997–2009
* Maarten Weyler 2009–2011

## Assistant Directors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Conservatory_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Assistant Directors")]

* [Karel Miry](/wiki/Karel_Miry "Karel Miry") 1871–1889

## Supervisors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Conservatory_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Supervisors")]

* Victor Van den Hecke de Lembeke 1855–1870
* Gustave Louis Marie De Burbure De Wesembeek 1870–1871

## Deans of the School of Arts, [University College Ghent](/wiki/Hogeschool_Gent "Hogeschool Gent")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Conservatory_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Deans of the School of Arts, University College Ghent")]

* Wim de Temmerman 2011–2019
* Lars Kwakkenbos 2019–2020
* Filip Rathé 2021–present
